upload_desc: Drag and drop your PBIP folder (as ZIP) or click to browse
upload_hint: ZIP your PBIP project folder or upload a .pbix file directly
grade_A: Excellent. The report follows best practices with minimal issues.
grade_B: Good. Some improvements recommended but no critical issues.
grade_C: Fair. Several areas need attention for better performance and maintainability.
grade_D: Poor. Significant issues detected that likely affect performance and reliability.
grade_F: Critical. Major structural problems require immediate attention.
no_findings: No issues found in this category!
error_not_zip: File must be a ZIP or PBIX archive
error_no_pbip: No valid PBIP project found in the ZIP. Make sure to ZIP the project folder containing .Report and .SemanticModel subfolders.
kpi_suggestions_desc: Based on your data model, these KPIs could be valuable but were not found as existing measures.
dax_improvements_desc: These existing DAX measures can be rewritten for better performance or readability.
no_suggestions: Your model looks great! No additional KPIs to suggest.
exec_summary_template: Your report contains {tables} tables, {measures} measures, and {relationships} relationships. The analysis identified {findings} findings. {attention}
exec_summary_no_issues: No critical issues detected. The model follows good practices.
history_empty: No analyses saved yet. Analyze a project to get started.
unused_measures_desc: Measures not referenced by any visual or other measure.
lineage_desc: Dependency tree showing how measures reference each other.
upload_multiple: Upload multiple PBIP projects for comparison
DAX-001: Avoid nesting CALCULATE inside another CALCULATE. Extract the inner CALCULATE into a separate measure or use filter arguments on a single CALCULATE.
DAX-002: Replace {func}({table}, {table}[{col}]) with {alt}({table}[{col}]). Simple column aggregations do not need an iterator.
DAX-003: Use VAR to store repeated subexpressions. This improves both readability and performance by evaluating the expression only once.
DAX-004: The IF function is redundant when it returns TRUE/FALSE or 1/0. Use the condition directly, or wrap it with NOT() for the inverse case.
DAX-005: Replace COUNTROWS(VALUES(Table[Column])) with DISTINCTCOUNT(Table[Column]) for simplicity.
DAX-006: This measure is complex but does not use variables (VAR/RETURN). Breaking it into intermediate variables improves readability.
DAX-007: This measure has no format string defined. Add an explicit format string (e.g., '#,##0', '0.00%', '$#,##0.00').
DAX-008: Measure/column name does not follow recommended naming conventions. Use descriptive Title Case names.
DAX-009: Avoid using FILTER on an entire table inside CALCULATE. Use direct filter arguments for simple conditions.
DAX-010: Replace IF(ISBLANK(...), default, value) with COALESCE(value, default) for cleaner code.
DAX-011: Use the DIVIDE function instead of the / operator to safely handle division by zero.
PQ-001: Step uses {func} which typically breaks query folding. Subsequent operations will be processed locally. Move this step as late as possible.
PQ-002: Table.Buffer forces the entire table into memory, breaking query folding and lazy evaluation. Remove it unless strictly needed.
PQ-003: A sort step appears before a filter step. Move filtering before sorting to reduce rows sorted and preserve query folding.
PQ-004: Connection strings and file paths should use Power Query parameters instead of hardcoded values for portability.
PQ-005: This query has {count} steps (threshold: {threshold}). Consider consolidating or splitting into smaller reusable queries.
PQ-007: This query has {count} type conversion steps. Consolidate them into a single step at the end of your query.
DM-001: Bidirectional cross-filtering on '{from_table}' to '{to_table}'. This can cause ambiguous results and performance issues. Use single-direction unless strictly required.
DM-002: Many-to-many relationship between '{from_table}' and '{to_table}'. Consider introducing a bridge table.
DM-003: Table '{table}' is not connected to any other table. It will not respond to filters from other tables.
DM-004: No dedicated date table detected. A proper date table is essential for time intelligence (YTD, MTD, YoY).
DM-005: Calculated column '{column}' in '{table}' uses aggregation function '{func}'. This should be a measure instead.
DM-006: Snowflake pattern: '{table}' acts as both lookup target and child in a chain. Consider denormalizing into star schema.
DM-007: Column '{table}[{column}]' may have high cardinality. If not used for analysis, consider removing it to reduce model size.
DM-008: Mixed storage modes detected: {modes}. Ensure this is intentional.
DM-010: Fact table '{table}' has no measures defined. Consider adding measures for numeric columns.
RP-001: Page '{page}' has {count} visuals (threshold: {threshold}). Consider reducing visuals or using drillthrough pages.
RP-002: Report has {count} pages (threshold: {threshold}). Consider splitting into multiple focused reports.
RP-003: Visual type '{type}' on page '{page}' is resource-intensive. Minimize its data volume.
RP-004: Visual on page '{page}' has {count} filters (threshold: {threshold}). Consider simplifying or moving filters to page level.
RP-005: Page '{page}' appears to be tooltip/drillthrough but is not hidden. Set visibility to Hidden.
RP-006: Visual on page '{page}' is missing alternative text. Add alt text for accessibility.
SEC-001: Table '{table}' has no RLS filter while {count} other tables are protected. Consider adding row-level security.
SEC-002: Role '{role}' has no table permissions defined. Remove empty roles or add filter expressions.
SEC-003: RLS filter on '{table}' uses expensive function '{func}'. This may impact query performance for all users.
SEC-004: Column '{table}[{col}]' appears to contain {type} data but is not hidden. Hide sensitive columns to prevent data exposure.
SEC-005: Data source for '{table}' uses unencrypted HTTP connection. Switch to HTTPS for secure data transfer.
SEC-006: Potential credentials found in source expression for '{table}'. Use parameters or environment variables instead.
SEC-007: Table '{table}' data is exposed without any RLS or page-level filtering.
security_desc: Data exposure and security risk analysis
NC-001: Measure '{name}' does not follow {convention} naming convention.
NC-002: Column '{col}' in '{table}' uses inconsistent naming compared to other columns in the same table.
NC-003: Table '{table}' name contains special characters, leading digits, or extra spaces.
PERF-001: Model is not a star schema. Table '{table}' appears on both the one-side and many-side of relationships. Consider restructuring to a star schema.
PERF-002: Model has {count} bidirectional relationships. This can severely impact performance. Use single-direction where possible.
PERF-003: Table '{table}' has {count} columns. Consider removing unused columns to reduce model size and improve refresh performance.
PERF-004: Calculated column '{col}' in '{table}' references other tables via aggregation. Convert to a measure for better performance.
PERF-005: Table '{table}' uses DirectQuery while {count} tables use Import. Mixed modes add latency to queries involving both.
BK-001: Bookmark '{name}' is defined but not linked to any visual action or button.
BK-003: Report has {count} bookmarks (threshold: {threshold}). Consider reducing to improve maintainability.
diff_upload_desc: Upload two versions of the same PBIP project to see changes
light_mode_desc: PBIX Light Mode — Report and Power Query analysis only. For full analysis (DAX, model, performance), export your report as PBIP format.
pbix_upload_note: You can also upload .pbix files for a light analysis (report + Power Query only)
editor_no_source: Editor not available. Re-upload as PBIP ZIP for editing capabilities.
